verses.text: Paul, 'a servant of God,' to Titus, mine own son after the common faith; grace and peace. Greeting. He writes to his fellow-worker in Crete.
Paul, 'a servant of God,' to Titus, mine own son after the common faith; grace and peace. Greeting. He writes to his fellow-worker in Crete.
Paul, 'a servant of God,' to Titus, mine own son after the common faith; grace and peace. Greeting. He writes to his fellow-worker in Crete.
Paul, 'a servant of God,' to Titus, mine own son after the common faith; grace and peace. Greeting. He writes to his fellow-worker in Crete.
I left thee to 'ordain elders' in every city, blameless, 'the husband of one wife.' Set in order. Each church needs faithful elders.
I left thee to 'ordain elders' in every city, blameless, 'the husband of one wife.' Set in order. Each church needs faithful elders.
A bishop must be blameless, not selfwilled, 'holding fast the faithful word,' able to convince gainsayers. Sound and able. He must hold and defend the truth.
A bishop must be blameless, not selfwilled, 'holding fast the faithful word,' able to convince gainsayers. Sound and able. He must hold and defend the truth.
A bishop must be blameless, not selfwilled, 'holding fast the faithful word,' able to convince gainsayers. Sound and able. He must hold and defend the truth.
There are many vain talkers, 'whose mouths must be stopped,' teaching for gain. Beware. Deceivers must be silenced.
There are many vain talkers, 'whose mouths must be stopped,' teaching for gain. Beware. Deceivers must be silenced.
There are many vain talkers, 'whose mouths must be stopped,' teaching for gain. Beware. Deceivers must be silenced.
Rebuke them sharply, 'that they may be sound in the faith,' not heeding fables. Firm rebuke. Error is met with plain correction.
Rebuke them sharply, 'that they may be sound in the faith,' not heeding fables. Firm rebuke. Error is met with plain correction.
To the pure all things are pure; they 'profess that they know God; but in works they deny him.' Empty profession. Words without works deny the Lord.
